meta_title: For Students: idea validation to Solve can't debug code (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to idea validation and overcome can't debug code. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Idea Validation to Solve Debugging Roadblocks
content.seo_description: Overcome debugging burnout by validating your ideas early. Use this advanced checklist with Antigravity and Claude Code to build faster, smarter, and better.
content.answer_block: Debugging isn't just about syntax; it's often a symptom of poor architectural assumptions. By rigorous idea validation, you prune non-viable features before they become technical debt. Use Antigravity to simulate logic flows and Claude Code to automate refactoring, ensuring your codebase remains clean, modular, and fundamentally bug-free.
content.intro:
The screen glows with a labyrinth of red error messages, and the silence of your room feels heavy with the frustration of a broken build. You aren't just stuck; you're trapped in a cycle of endless patches that never resolve the core architectural failure. This is the 'beginner's wall'—where the complexity of your ambition outpaces your current ability to trace execution flows.

But here is the secret: senior engineers don't just 'get better at debugging.' They build systems that are inherently easier to reason about. By shifting your focus from fixing broken code to validating the viability and architecture of your ideas before you commit to the logic, you transform your development process from a reactive chore into a proactive engineering masterclass.

steps.title: Define the Minimal Viable Architecture
steps.description: Before writing code, map out your data flow. If you can't explain the state transitions, you can't debug them.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this project idea: [Insert Idea]. Break down the core data state transitions and identify three potential architectural failure points.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review my architectural diagram for potential race conditions or state management flaws.'

steps.title: Automated Logic Verification
steps.description: Use Antigravity to simulate your logic against edge cases before implementation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a stress-test simulation for this module: [Insert Module]. Generate edge cases where the logic might fail or return unexpected nulls.

steps.title: Refactoring via Incremental Validation
steps.description: Break complex functions into testable units to isolate where bugs manifest.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this function into pure, side-effect-free components for easier unit testing.
steps.claude_code_command: claude refactor [File_Path] --goal 'decouple business logic from UI components'

steps.title: Predictive Error Mapping
steps.description: Anticipate runtime errors by mapping every possible failure branch in your code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Map the error propagation path for this service. Identify where exceptions might bubble up unhandled.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Generate a comprehensive error-handling wrapper for this async function.'

steps.title: Final Integration Stress Test
steps.description: Synthesize your validated modules and run a final automated integration check.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a final audit of the integrated code. Ensure data consistency across the entire flow.

pro_tips: Treat every error message as a documentation request; ask Claude Code to explain the 'why' behind the exception, not just the fix.
If you find yourself debugging for more than 45 minutes, your architecture is likely flawed; delete the module and rewrite it using a different pattern.
Use Antigravity to generate 'negative test cases'—code that intentionally tries to break your input validation.
Maintain a 'bug journal' to spot patterns; if you keep getting 'undefined' errors, you need to implement stricter type checking (TypeScript) or schema validation.
Always commit your state *before* a major refactor so you can use Claude Code to perform 'diff-based debugging' to see exactly what broke.
content.next_steps: Once your code is stable, deploy your MVP to a staging environment and conduct a 'user stress test' to see if your architectural assumptions hold up in the wild.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the strategy; now it is time to build the future. Stop letting bugs dictate your progress and start engineering with intent. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
